paragraphs.body: Possessing the logo of "Threads" for over a decade, Threads Software Limited, a small company in the UK, has warned Meta of future lawsuits about using the name. Proving an "official press release", the company's managing director, John Yardley, mentioned "Taking on a U.S. $150 billion company is not an easy decision for us to make. Our business now faces a serious threat from one of the largest technology companies in the world." By the time Meta's Threads was launched in July 2023, Threads Software's Facebook account was found closed. [1]

Threads Software Ltd.'s Point of View[edit] The company claimed that the "threads.app" domain was not for sale, and refused Meta's bid. Threads Software Ltd. informed Meta of requesting "an injunction in the UK High Court" except if Meta stopped using the name "Threads". John Yardley, the company's managing director, said that Meta planned to "incur legal costs" just to keep the rights of Threads Software Ltd.'s existence. Moreover, the actions could help Meta postpone the litigation.[2]

The reality of the company under Zuckerberg's perspective[edit] Zuckerberg mentioned that Meta owns much more money and lawyers, making it hard for the small company to win the battle in the U.S. compared to the British court. He also said that "part of that is because the U.S. has more rules surrounding how you make a determination of an infringement. It’s less likely that in the United States that anyone has heard of this small British company." Zuckerberg made the point that it has a low chance of hurting Meta. He believes that the users of Meta's Threads will continue to use the app with the updating of more and more functions and features.[1]

Meta's Point of View[edit] Meta stated that "Threads Software 'does not appear to be software for the extraction of business information and knowledge'." In addition, "the trademark has been registered for more than five years it can be removed from the register on the grounds of non-use" was also mentioned.[2]

The growth of Meta's Threads and the problem it faced[edit] On July 5, 2023, Meta's Threads was officially opened to the public. The new app was "launched as a competitor to X, formerly known as Twitter." [1] Instagram users could access the new app by logging in to their accounts. Over 100 million people started to use the app within five days.[3] Meanwhile, the app is still inaccessible in Europe, waiting for the European Commission to go through "data policies" and furthermore, give out the "regulatory clearance".[1]

Threads Software Ltd.[edit] Located in Surbiton, England, the software company aims to support business communication with an organized dashboard. Threads provide help "From intelligent search functions to phone call transcription and HubSpot integration".[4]

Threads (Message hub)[edit] Provided by Threads Software Ltd., Threads is an "intelligent message hub" and was "convinced and trademarked in 2012 by JPY Ltd." The taking of the name "Threads" by Meta would highly lead to confusion.[2]

1. "Meta faces legal action over Threads name". Annenberg Media. 2023-11-02. Retrieved 2025-02-26. 2. Peet, Ellen (2024-02-29). "Threads Software v. Meta: trademark dispute sends small business into a spin". Trademark Lawyer Magazine. Retrieved 2025-02-26. 3. "Threads: The inside story of Meta's newest social app". Engineering at Meta. 2023-09-07. Retrieved 2025-02-26. 4. "Shared Inbox Software For Teams - Threads Intelligent Message Hub". Threads. Retrieved 2025-02-26.
